ety: for A. خيمة ḵẖaimat; fr. خيم

s.f. Drawing, dragging, pulling; draw, pull; attraction; drawing aside or apart (from), holding aloof (from); aversion, &c. ( = khīć, q.v.); procrastination, delay; — tenseness, tension; — scantiness; scarcity, deficiency
more: s. f. 1. Pulling, drawing, draw, attaction, pull. 2. Scarcity. کهينچ کهميٿ khaiṅch-khameṭ, s. (dakh.) Contention, struggle: see کشمکش kash-ma-kash.
subs: or khaiṅć-ā-khāṅćī, or khaiṅć-ā-khaiṅć, or khaiṅć-ā-khaiṅćī, or (dialec.) khaiṅć-khameṭ, s.f. Pulling and hauling; stretching and straining; struggle, contention, scuffle.

def: v.t. = khiṅćānā = khićānā, q.v. v.t. = khićānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to pull or draw; to cause to stretch or tighten, &c., &c.; — to pull, draw; to stretch, &c. ( = khīṅćnā, q.v.).
s. f. Pulling and hawling, struggle, scuffle.
s. f. Pulling and hawling, struggle, scuffle.

ety: khaiṅć˚ = Prk. कच्छ(इ) = S. क्रक्ष्य(ति), future of rt. कृष् (but used in the sense of the pres.)
def: v.t. To draw, drag, pull; to attract, to draw in, suck in, absorb (gen. khaiṅć-lenā); to draw out, to stretch; to extract; to pull off, strip off (the skin, &c.); to draw tight, to tighten; to hang (on a cross, or a gibbet; — gen. khaiṅć-denā); — to draw away or aside (from), to hold aloof ( = khićnā, q.v.); to withdraw, withhold; — to draw (a sword, or a bill, or a line or figure); to delineate, to sketch; to paint; — to drag out, to endure, suffer, bear
more: v. a. 1. See اينچنا with phrases. 2. ( khacheṛnā ) To drag along. 3. To extract; distil; brew. 4. To write out; draw a bill.
(causal of کهچنا) v. a. To pull, draw, tighten, strip off, flay, delineate, paint, endure, suffer. کهينچ لينا khaiṅch lenā, To pull or draw out, extract, extort.
subs: v.t. (intens.), To pull or draw out, to extract; to extort; to pull or drag away; to drag along; to pull or draw in, to absorb, &c.
v.t. (intens.) To pull or drag out, &c.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀔𑀺𑀁𑀘𑀇 (*khiṃcaï), 𑀔𑀁𑀘𑀇 (khaṃcaï, “to pull”), from a family of Prakrit words with "dragging" connotations of unknown further origin. Compare Punjabi ਖਿੱਚਣਾ (khiccaṇā) / کِھچّݨا (khiccṇā), Gujarati ખેંચવું (kheñcvũ), Marathi खेचणे (kheċṇe), Bengali খেঁচা (khẽca).

def: v.t. = khiṅćwānā = khićwānā, q.v. v.t. = khićwānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be drawn or pulled, &c.; to cause to be stretched, or tightened, &c. (see khićnā)
more: v. a. caus. of کهينچنا q. v.

ety: inf. n. of خير 'to be possessed of good'; 'to be good or well'
def: s.f. Good; goodness; benefit; good fortune, prosperity, welfare, well-being, weal; health; happiness; — adj. & adv. Good; best; well; — well; very well; it is just as well; no matter; — intj. Indeed! you don't say so!
more: adv. 1. Well; very well. 2. Be it so; never mind ( jāne do ); it is of no consequence; very good; yes; that will do! khair ; H. sach kahnā yā kaho , intj. Indeed! you dont say so! 2. It is well; never mind. khair, khairi'yat , n. f. 1. Good; welfare. 1. Apnī khair chāhtā hai to chalā jā! 2. Jab kahā maiṅ ne ki 'tum āë, isī meṅ huī khair,' Hāë! kis nāz se farmāyā 'ki tū kyā kartā? ' Nazīr. And when I said to her 'you've come—tis well for you,' Ah! with what archness she then said 'what could you do?' 2. Health; happiness. khair-andesh , n. m. One who looks to another's welfare. khair-khuāh , n. 1. A well-wisher; friend. 2. A common signature to an anonymous petition or address. khair-khuāh-i-sarkār , n. m. Loyal people. khair-khuāhī, khair-andeshī , n. f. Good will or intention; benevolence; friendship. khair-sallāh, khair o āfiyat ; H. khem kusal , n. f. Health and prosperity; welfare. k…

1. adj. Good, best, well. 2. adv. Well. 3. s. f. Goodness, good, health, happiness. خيرالنسا ḵẖairu-n-nisā, The best of women, Fāt̤ima. خيرانديش ḵẖair-andesh, A wellwisher, a friend. خيرانديشي ḵẖair-andesh-ī, s. f. Good will, benevolence, good intention. خيرباد ḵẖair-bād, May he prosper. خيرخبر ḵẖair ḵẖabar (simply) News. خيرخواه ḵẖair-ḵẖẉāh, Well-wishing, benevolent; a friend, a wellwisher. خيرخواهي ḵẖair-ḵẖẉāh-ī, s. f. Good will, wishing well, friendship, benevolence. خيروعافيت ḵẖair-o-āﻌfiyat, s. f. Health, welfare.

def: s.f. A kind of silk cloth stained with waving marks like the traces of the old leaves on the trunk of the date-tree
more: n. f. S. खर्जूर; Pr. khajjūr ; Pālī, khajjurī . 1. A date, or date tree; Phœnix dactylifera , Wat. 2. A sweetmeat made in the form of a date. Khajūreṅ talo aur bachche kā dūdh chhuṛā do! Mah. khajūr-chhaṛī , n. f. A silk cloth with tracings of date leaves. khajūr'ī , adj. Matted; plaited. Khajūri woh choṭī, zarī kā mubāf . Mīr Hasan.

s. m. 1. A date, or date-tree. (Phœnix dactylifera). 2. Wild date. (Elate silvestris: Linn. Phœnix silvestris: Roxb. ). 3. A kind of sweetmeat. کهجور چهڙي khajūr-chhaṛī, A kind of silk cloth stained with waving marks like the traces of the old leaves on the trunk of the date tree.
subs: s.m. The juice or extract of the wild date (tāṛī), used (when in a state of fermentation) to leaven bread, and as an intoxicating liquor.

s.f. Dust, earth; ashes; — little, precious little, none at all, nothing whatever (e.g. to kām kyā ḵẖāk hotā hogā, 'What precious little work will be doing'; ḵẖāk jāntā hai, 'He knows nothing whatever'); — intj. Out (upon)! away (with)! a fig (for)! &c. (e.g. ḵẖāk aisī zindagī-par)
more: n. f. Dust ( dhūl ); earth (Sant. ot hasa ); ashes. 1. Tumhāre mare des pāk, hamāre mare des khāk . Prov. Your death would purify the land, mine me to dust remand. 2. Tum khāk kī chuṭkī bhī uṭhā doge to mujhe eteqād hai . Even a pinch of dust from your hand I would swallow in faith. (Said to a physician). 3. Gar ek ko hazār rupaë kā milā kafan, Aur ek yoṅ paṛā rahā be-kas, barahna tan, Kīṛe, makoṛe khā gaë donoṅ ke tan badan, Dekhā jo ham ne, āh! to sach hai yehī sukhan, 'Jo khāk se banā hai woh ākhir ko khāk hai .' Nazīr. What though one's coffin for rupees one thousand's made, The while another naked friendless on the ground is laid, A prey to worms both are alike, I saw and said, 'Alas! but dust ye were, to dust ye are returned!' 4. Woh to ye samjhī dil meṅ, ki 'dhelā maiṅ pāūṅgī. Damṛī ke pān, damṛī kī missī maṅgāūṅgī, Bāqī rahe chhai dām so pānī bharāaṅgī,' Phir dil meṅ sochtī hai, …

s. f. Dust, earth; ashes. خاکبازي ḵẖāk-bāz-ī, s. f. Playing with dust. خاکپا ḵẖāk-pā, s. f. Dust of the feet, earth trodden under foot, the lowest or most worthless thing. خاک پهانکنا ḵẖāk phānknā, To wander, to stray. خاک توده ḵẖāk-toda, s. m. A butt or mark of earth for shooting arrows at. خاک چهاننا ḵẖāk-chhānnā (lit. To sift the dust) expresses excessive labour and exertion in fruitless search. خاک ڐالني ḵẖāk-ḍālnī, is used to express the concealing of those things from the disclosure of which disgrace may be expected. خاکروب ḵẖāk-rob, s. m. A sweeper. خاک روبه ḵẖāk-rob-a, s. m. Sweepings. خاک روبي ḵẖāk-rob-ī, s. f. Sweeping. خاکسار ḵẖāk-sār, adj. Base, mean, low-born, humble. خاکساري ḵẖāk-sār-ī, s. f. Meanness, humbleness. خاک سرپراڙانا ḵẖāk sir par uṛānā, To lament. خاک سياه کرنا ḵẖāk siyāh karnā, To plunder and lay waste a country, to depredate. خاک شو ḵẖāk-sho, s. m. 1. A person …

To throw dust, to raise a dust; to wander, roam; — to make a stir or commotion; to defame
v.n. Dust to be blowing; to be clouded with dust; — (fig.) to be reduced to poverty; to be disgraced
s.f. Dust of the feet; earth trodden under foot; the lowest or most worthless thing
To wander, to stray; — to deviate (from truth), to lie
s.m. A pile or mound of earth; a butt or mark of earth (for shooting arrows at); a sand-hill
To lick the dust, to humble oneself
'To sift dust,' to labour or exert oneself to no purpose, to go through laborious and fruitless toil or search; to beat the air
s.m. A receptacle or pit for dust, earth, ashes, &c.; a place where rubbish is placed, a dust-bin; — (met.) the world
(-par), To throw dust (on); to bury, to conceal (an affair, or any thing disgraceful); — to heap curses (on), to execrate; — ḵẖāk-rob, s.m. A sweeper
adj. Earth-born; produced from or made of dust
adj. 'Like dust'; base, mean, low-born, humble, lowest of the low; — s.m. Humble petitioner (used by one in speaking of himself)
s.f. Humility, self-abasement, humbleness, lowness, meanness
To throw dust and ashes on the head, to mourn, lament
(-kī), To plunder and lay waste a country, to depredate
ḵẖāk-shafā, s.f. 'Earth of healing'; sacred earth or ashes from a shrine
s.m. A person in the mint who washes the ashes to recover any particles of bullion which may have fallen; — the man who prepares earth for brick-making or pottery
v.t., and ḵẖāk-meṅ milānā, v.t. To reduce to dust and ashes, to level with the ground, to waste, ruin demolish
v.n. To mingle with the dust, to die, to perish; to be laid waste, be ruined, be demolished
v.n. To become or be reduced to dust; to be ruined ( = ḵẖāk-meṅ milnā); to moulder or pine away; to be consumed (with rage, or jealousy, &c.).

more: v. (dakh.) To hawk (spit up, Harris ): see کهنکهارنا khankhārnā.

def: s. A receptacle or pit for dust, earth, ashes, &c.; ( met. ) the world.
n. f. Trial; trouble; difficulty. khakeṛ uṭhānā , v. n. To undergo trouble or labour.
s.f. Hurrying; bustle; labour, difficulty, trouble, trial
v.n. To hurry, to bustle to and fro; to use strenuous effort.

Dust, earth; ashes; — little, precious little, none at all, nothing whatever (e.g. to kām kyā ḵẖāk hotā hogā, 'What precious little work will be doing'; ḵẖāk jāntā hai, 'He knows nothing whatever'); — intj. Out (upon)! away (with)! a fig (for)! &c. (e.g. ḵẖāk aisī zindagī-par)

To wander, to stray; — to deviate (from truth), to lie
s.m. A pile or mound of earth; a butt or mark of earth (for shooting arrows at); a sand-hill
To lick the dust, to humble oneself
'To sift dust,' to labour or exert oneself to no purpose, to go through laborious and fruitless toil or search; to beat the air
s.m. A receptacle or pit for dust, earth, ashes, &c.; a place where rubbish is placed, a dust-bin; — (met.) the world
(-par), To throw dust (on); to bury, to conceal (an affair, or any thing disgraceful); — to heap curses (on), to execrate; — ḵẖāk-rob, s.m. A sweeper
adj. Earth-born; produced from or made of dust
adj. 'Like dust'; base, mean, low-born, humble, lowest of the low; — s.m. Humble petitioner (used by one in speaking of himself)
s.f. Humility, self-abasement, humbleness, lowness, meanness
To throw dust and ashes on the head, to mourn, lament
(-kī), To plunder and lay waste a country, to depredate
ḵẖāk-shafā, s.f. 'Earth of healing'; sacred earth or ashes from a shrine
s.m. A person in the mint who washes the ashes to recover any particles of bullion which may have fallen; — the man who prepares earth for brick-making or pottery
v.t., and ḵẖāk-meṅ milānā, v.t. To reduce to dust and ashes, to level with the ground, to waste, ruin demolish

v.n. To become or be reduced to dust; to be ruined ( = ḵẖāk-meṅ milnā); to moulder or pine away; to be consumed (with rage, or jealousy, &c.).

def: s.m. = khaṅkhār, q.v. s.m. Pit, shaft (of a mine).

s.m. A kind of rattle used in breaking in a horse (it is formed of a small board, shaped somewhat like a bleacher's beetle or mall, on each side of the flat part of which three or four rows of rattles are fixed and suspended; by an occasional shake of this, one of the horsebreaker's assistants who follows the horse with it in hand, helps to break in the horse, and to give him his proper paces).
more: n. m. A rattle used in breaking in a horse.

def: s.f. Hurrying; bustle; labour, difficulty, trouble, trial
v.n. To hurry, to bustle to and fro; to use strenuous effort.
s.f. A hollow in a tree, &c.).
n. f. A hollow in a tree, etc.

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. Prk. खुड˚ = S. खण्ड्; cf. also koṛnā
def: v.t. To scrape, scratch, rake up; to scoop out; — to make close search for, to search out.
more: v. a. To scoop, to scrape.

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. Prk. खुड˚ = S. खण्ड्; cf. also koṛnā
def: v.t. To scrape, scratch, rake up; to scoop out; — to make close search for, to search out.

adj. Of the dust, of ashes; earthy; earthly, terrestrial; dusty; dust-coloured; — s.f. The colour of dust or earth; — s.m. A Hindū mendicant who smears his body with the ashes of burnt cowdung; — an earthy-brown dye; cloth of the colour of dust or earth (of which the uniform of the Sikh regiments is made; hence), Sikh infantry; — an epithet of a kind of arrow; — unirrigated land (depending wholly on rain)
more: adj. 1. Dusty; of the color of dust or earth. 2. Earthy; earthly; terrestrial. khākī , n. m. 1. An earthy brown dye. 2. A Hin. mendicant who smears his body with the ashes of burnt cowdung. 3. The uniform of Panjābī soldiers, hence Sikh infantry. 4. Unirrigated land depending wholly on rain. khākī anḍā , n. m. See under anḍā .
adj. 1. Dusty. 2. Earthy, earthly; of ashes. 3. The epithet of a kind of arrow. خاکي انڐا ḵẖākī anḍā, s. m. 1. An addle egg, windegg. 2. A term of reproach.
subs: s.m. An addled egg, wind egg; a term of reproach; bastard.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خاکی (xākī).

def: Earth of healing, ( met, for) earth of karbalā: see Qanoon-e-islam, gloss. p. xcix.
s.f. Ashes; calx (of metals)
more: s. f. Ashes, calx. خاکسترکرنا ḵẖākistar karnā, v. a. To calcine. خاکسترهونا ḵẖākistar honā, v. n. To calcine.
subs: (-kī), To reduce to ashes, to calcine: ḵẖākistar honā, v.n. To be reduced to ashes; to be calcined.

def: adj. Covered with ashes; ashy; ash-coloured.
more: adj. Of the colour of ashes, covered with ashes, ashy.

def: s.m. pl. (of ḵẖākī), A denomination of Mohammadan faqīrs.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Decrepit, infirm, bent or bowed (with age); worn out (with age); — a decrepit old man; — an elder, a senior, a chief.
more: n. m. See خاکي Tum kyā kahīṅ ke khakkā sāhib ho? Are you the laird? (Are you anybody in particular?).
adj. (dakh.) Wise, cunning, old: see ديرينه der-īna.

def: isthmus
wety: Probably coined after آبنای (âbnây), from a compound of خاک (xâk, “earth, dirt”) + نای (nây, “lane, throat, pipe”).

v.t. (dialec.) = khakhornā, q.v. v.t. To scrape, scratch, rake up; to scoop out; — to make close search for, to search out.
more: Tir. v. a. 1. See kurednā . 2. To search out; leave no stone unturned.

def: first-person singular subjective personal pronoun: I, I humbly, this servant
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَاکْسَار (xāksār, literally “earth-like”). By surface analysis, خَاک (xāk) + ـسَار (-sār).

def: dative/accusative of خاکْسار (xāksār)
ergative of خاکْسار (xāksār)
s.f. A small weight (the seed of Abrus precatorius) used by goldsmiths (syn. ghuṅgćī); — a red bramble; — a small red seed used in medicine (chiefly as an eye-salve).
more: s. f. 1. A small weight used by goldsmiths (seed of Abrus precatorius). 2. A red bramble. 3. A small red seed used in medicine.

def: s.f. corr. of next, q.v.
more: n. f. A small red seed used in medicine, chiefly as an eye-salve. (?)

def: s.m. Maternal uncle, mother's brother; — a black mole on the face (regarded as ornamental); a spot, patch (natural); an artificial spot (made of kājal, &c., for ornament, or to ward off the effects of the malignant eye)
more: n. m. 1. A mole on the face. Khāl, gore rukh pe uske, dil ko lete haiṅ churā, Chāṅdnī meṅ chor paṛte haiṅ, ajab andher hai! That jet black mole on face so fair hath stole my heart away, For thief to light on moon so bright strange outrage in midday! 2. A beauty spot made for ornament. khāl khāl , adv. 1. Very few. khāl khāl ādmī the . There were very few men. 2. Here and there.

s. m. 1. A mole on the face (black and ornamental). 2. An artificial spot made for ornament. 3. A maternal uncle, motherʼs brother. خال خال ḵẖāl-ḵẖāl, adj. 1. Thick, close, thickly spread. 2. Little, scarce. 3. adv. Rarely, here and there.
subs: adj. & adv. Thick, close, thickly spread; — little, scarce, rare; — very few, few and far between, here and there; rarely.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خال (xāl), from Arabic خال (ḵāl).

ety: inf. n. of خلع 'to pull or throw off,' &c.
def: s.m. Pulling off, stripping off, stripping; deposing, removing (from office)
more: (Heb. ) verbal noun, Stripping off, stripping, deposing, removing. خلع العذار ḵẖalaʼaﻌ-l-iﻌẕār (Has put off the rein) Is unchecked or uncontrouled.
subs: lit. 'He has thrown off the rein'; is unchecked or uncontrolled.

def: s.f. (m.?) A creek, bay, gulf; inlet (of the sea, or of a large river); — a canal, rivulet, watercourse, river; — a moat, trench.
more: n. f. S. खल्ल 1. A hide ( chamṛā) . 1. Mainā jo 'maiṅ-nā kahe dūdh bhāt nit khāë, Bakrī jo 'maiṅ maiṅ' kare ulṭī khāl khichāë . Prov. Said to a boaster. The mainā 'not me' cries, he's fed with milk and rice, The goat cries out 'I, I' his skin's off in a trice. 2. Je ghar ranḍvā apne hoto, Dārī! khūnṭī pe khāl ṭakī rahtī, Ranḍvā ke bagaṛ māṅ kyoṅ uchkī? Rus. song. If the widower had been within On the nail I would have hung your skin, Strumpet! what makes you peep into his yard? 2. Bellows ( dhauṅknī ). khāl-upāṛ ; pop. khal-upāṛ , n. m. lit . one who takes the skin off. A skin-flint; one who is exacting in money matters. khāl udheṛnā, kheṅchnā, yā uṛānā , v. n. See chamṛī udheṛnā . khāl bigaṛnā , v. n. To be puffed up. Kahīṅ khāl to nahīṅ bigaṛ gaī? khāl meṅ mast h. , v. n. See under apnī. Koī khāl meṅ mast, koī māl meṅ mast . Prov. Some in their skins (in themselves) intoxicate, some ar…
s. f. 1. Skin, hide. کهال کهينچنا khāl khainchnā, To take the skin off, to flay. 2. Bellows. 3. A rivulet, river, canal, creek, inlet.

ety: S. खल्लः(?); cf. ćhāl = S. शल्कं
def: s.f. Skin, hide; — bellows
subs: s.m. 'One who takes the skin off'; one who is exacting in money-matters; a skin-flint
or khāl udheṛnā, or khāl uṛānā (-kī), To take the skin off, to flay.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Low, base, mean, vile: malicious, mischievous; artful, bad, wicked; cruel; — a wicked or mischievous person, &c.; — s.m. A threshing-floor; a granary; a stock (of grain); a pit; — an entertainment; — deposit of oil, sediment, the cake that remains after the oil has been pressed out of certain seeds (esp. sesamum and sarsoṅ), oil-cake (com. khalī or kharī); — the plant Achyranthes lanata, the woolly Illecebrum or mountain knot-grass
more: adj. S. खल, Pālī, khalo . See اوچها (1, 7). 1. Shallow. 2. Mean; worthless (person).
n. f. Oil-cake. Khal khā! khal! (Said by shopkeepers to customers who want to buy cheap). khul h. , v. n. To crumble; moulder; decay. khalī , n. f. Scented oil-cake used as a hair wash.
s. 1. A worthless person, a malicious person. 2. An oil-cake. 3. A pit.
subs: (˚la+uk˚), a.f. Low or wicked language, abuse

def: (dakh.) To clank ( Harris ).
s.f. Crash (as of an earthen vessel in breaking to pieces).

ety: inf. n. of خلو 'to be empty or vacant,' &c.
def: adj. = ḵẖālī, q.v.; — s.m. A vacant place or space, vacancy, vacuity, vacuum
more: n. m. Vacuity; a vacuum; space. Khalā mahāl hai . Nature abhors a vacuum.
(Heb. ) s. m. Vacancy, vacuity. خلا ملا ḵẖalā-malā, Unfeigned friendship.
subs: adj. & s.m. Unfeigned, sincere; intimate, close (friendship); — sincere or un feigned friendship; warm or close attachment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خلاء (xalā'), from Arabic خَلَاء (ḵalāʔ).

def: s.m. A long pole with which a boat is impelled, an oar, a helm, a rudder (syn. patwār); — any sharp piercing thing (as a needle or awl): — wind in the bowels causing acute pain; a pain that comes suddenly in the side and joints; anything that passes away by degrees or slowly; — a written document signed by a judge (confirming any mercantile transaction, &c.); — trifles, idle talk, silly discourse.
more: s. A long pole with which they impel a boat, perhaps a rudder, trifles, idle talk, any thing which penetrates or goes down in a place, as a needle, &c., a wind causing sharp pain in the belly, a pain that comes suddenly in the side and joints, any thing that passes away by degrees or slowly, a written document signed by a judge.

def: s.m. A hole, hollow, pit, cavity: low land; a ravine; a creek, &c. ( = khāl, q.v.).
more: n. m. S. खल्ल 1. Low land full of ravines. 2. A rivulet; creek; inlet; watercourse; a gully.
s. f. 1. Skin, hide. کهال کهينچنا khāl khainchnā, To take the skin off, to flay. 2. Bellows. 3. A rivulet, river, canal, creek, inlet.

def: s.m. A granary, &c. (see khal).
astronaut
wety: خلا (xalā, “space”) + نورد (navard, “wanderer”), literally “space wanderer”

ety: for A. خالة, fem. of ḵẖāl
def: s.f. Maternal aunt, mother's sister
more: Hin. n. f. Mother's sister; maternal aunt. 1. Woh kyā merī khālā kā khal-bachchā lagtā hai jo maiṅ us kī pach karūṅ? Mah. Wom. 2. Khālā kā rutbā māṅ ke barābar . Prov. An aunt is as one's mother. khālā-zād bhāī, khalerā bhāī, mauserā bhāī , n. m. Mother's sister's son; cousin. khālā kā ghar, nānī jī kā ghar , One's aunt's house, hence a place of rest and security. 1. Āshqī khālā jī kā ghar nahīṅ hai . Prov. The paths of love are not as smooth as living in your aunt's house. ('The course of true love, etc.') 2. Piyeṅge masjid meṅ maë ko jākar, kisī kī khālā kā ghar nahīṅ hai; Kareṅge dilbar se chūmā-chāṭī, kisi ke bāvā kā ḍar nahīṅ hai . In mosque I'll have my wine, 'tis not your aunt's house, nor is 't his, My love I'll kiss and kiss all o'er, whose father dare bar this?

(properly, خاله) s. f. Maternal aunt, motherʼs sister. خالا کا يا خالا ماما کا گهريا خانهٴخالا ḵẖālā kā- or ḵẖālā māmā kā-ghar, or ḵẖāna-i-ḵẖālā (lit. The house of oneʼs aunt) signifies a place of rest and security.
subs: or ḵẖālā-māmā-kā ghar, or ḵẖāna-ě-ḵẖāla, s.m. 'Aunt's house'; a place of rest and security.

ety: for A. خالة, fem. of ḵẖāl
def: s.f. See ḵẖālā.
more: s. f. Motherʼs sister, maternal aunt.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَالَه (xāla), from Arabic خَالَة (ḵāla).

def: s.m. Mud, clay, mire, filth (cf. gil).
s.m. A substitute; a successor, an heir; a good son, a favourite son; a son; posterity, progeny, descendants; — adj. Filial, dutiful; — evil, depraved, corrupted
more: n. m. A son (Sant. hopan ); an heir; a successor; descendant. khalaf , adj. Filial; dutiful. Farzand wohī hai jo khalaf ho . Prov. A dutiful son is indeed a son. khalaf-ul-sidq, khalf-ur-rashīd ; H. sapūt , n. m. 1. A legitimate son. 2. The rightful heir. 3. A dutiful son. salaf se khalaf tak , adv. From father to son; from generation to generation. nā-khalaf ; H. kapūt , n. m. An undutiful son. Achchhe nā-khalaf paidā huë .
s. m. 1. A successour, an heir, a favourite son, posterity, descendants. 2. (adjectively) Depraved, corrupted. خلف الصدق ḵẖalafu-ṣ-ṣidḳ, True or rightful heir. ¶ ḵẖulf, Breach of promise.
subs: s.m. A legitimate son; a dutiful son; — true or rightful successor or heir

def: s. m. (dakh.) Motherʼs sisterʼs husband ( Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. iv.).
s.m. (local), Mother's sister's husband ( = ḵẖālū, q.v.). s.m. Maternal uncle, mother's sister's husband.
s.f. pl. (of ḵẖalq), Created things, creatures, the creation; men, people.

ety: khel + ait = Prk. आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन् (the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. augment āpi)
def: s.m. A gambler; — a pilferer; a pickpocket.

ety: v.n. fr. خلج 'to put in motion,' &c.
def: s.m. Throbbing, beating; distraction, agitation, anxiety, uneasiness
subs: (-kā), Anxiety or uneasiness to be removed.

ety: v.n. fr. خلّ 'to become lean, or poor,' &c.; 'to pierce,' &c.
def: s.m. Break, breach, chink, gap; hiatus; interruption; rupture; disorder, derangement, unsoundness, corruptness; confusion, disturbance; ruin; — flaw, defect, imperfection; damage, injury, harm, mischief, prejudice
more: n. m. خل a breach. 1. See اَرَ 6, 7. 2. Breach; rupture; misunderstanding. 3. Disturbance; confusion. 4. Damage; mischief; nuisance; prejudice; evil influence (of a spirit, etc.). Is ghar meṅ kuchh khalal hai . 5. Disorder; derangement. Is ke peṭ meṅ kuchh khalal hai. khalal-andāz , n. m. An intermeddler; a marplot; a disturber of the peace. khalal-andāz h. , v. n. 1. To interfere; meddle; interrupt. 2. To disturb; molest; impede. khalal-i-dimāg , n. m. Melancholy; madness. khalal k., yā ḍālnā , v. a. 1. To interfere; meddle; interrupt; disturb; impede; obstruct. 2. To shake; invalidate. 3. To injure; damage. haq meṅ khalal ḍāl'nā . To invalidate or bar a claim; to prejudice a right. be-khalal , adj. Uninterrupted; undisturbed.

(from خلّ) s. m. Interruption, disturbance, confusion, prejudice, damage, injury, defect, ruin, hiatus, breach. خلل دماغ ḵẖalal-i-dimāg̠ẖ, s. m. ( lit. Injury of the brain) Madness, melancholy, craziness. adj. Mad, hare-brained, hotheaded, brainsick, confused.
subs: (-meṅ), Disorder or disturbance, &c. to take place (in)
adj. & s.m. Introducing confusion, &c.; intermeddling; — one who introduces confusion, a disturber (of the peace, &c.); an intermeddler, a mar-plot
(-kā), v.n. To be an interrupter (of), to interrupt; to disturb; to interfere
s.m. Disorder of the brain, madness, craziness; melancholy
adj. Having a disordered brain, mad, crazy, confused, brain-sick, hare-brained; hot-headed

(-men), To interrupt, obstruct; to interfere, meddle; to disturb, mar, spoil, do injury (to), damage; to pervert, to turn aside (from); to impute corruptness (to); to shake, to invalidate; to prejudice (a right, &c.)
adj. & adv. Unbroken, uninterrupted; undisturbed; uninjured, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خلل (xalal), from Arabic خَلَل (ḵalal).

def: s.m. (Dakkhinī) The clattering or loud rippling of water; — adj. Wet through, drenched; weltering.
s.m. (Dakkhinī) A clattering stream; — a fountain.

ety: for A. خلالة, prob. n. of un. fr. ḵẖilāl, inf. n. iii of خلّ; see ḵẖullat
def: s.f. True or sincere friendship (in which is no ḵẖalal or defect).
more: s. f. Sincere friendship. ¶ ḵẖulālat, Bits of meat sticking between the teeth.

def: s.m. Interrupter; disturber, mischief-maker, &c. ( = ḵẖalal-andāz, q.v.).
s.m. A park, chase; pleasure-ground.

ety: S. खल्ल, prob. for खण्ड+क+रः; cf. khālā
def: s.m. A hollow, cavity, pit; hollow ground, low land; — bottom.
s.f. (dialec.), A pit, &c.; a place where salt is boiled.
more: s. The place in which salt is boiled (in Beng.).

ety: inf. n. of خلص 'to be free from difficulty,' &c.
def: s.m. Deliverance, liberation, release, discharge, acquittal; freedom; manumission, emancipation; — exemption, immunity; — adj. Free, liberated, redeemed; done, out
more: n. m. خلص he freed himself. Emancipation; manumission ( āzādī ). khalās , adj. Free; liberated ( āzād , 1, 2.). khalās-patr , n. A deed of release; an order of discharge; deed of dissolution. khalās k., khalāsī denā , v. a. To discharge; acquit ( āzād k .) khalās h. , v. n. 1. To be freed or set at liberty ( āzād h .). 2. To calve; to be brought to bed. 3. Semen emittere. Hotī hūṅ khalās, ab to yehī jān gaī maiṅ, Ek aur bhī dhakkā, tere qurbān gaī māin! Nazīr.

(from خلص Heb. ) s. m. Deliverance, liberation; used adjectively, Free, liberated, redeemed, done, out. خلاص کرنا ḵẖalāṣ karnā, To release, liberate, free, discharge. خلاص هونا ḵẖalāṣ honā, 1. To be freed or set at liberty, to be released or discharged, to emit. 2. To calve, to be brought to bed. 3. Emissio seminis, semen emittere.
subs: s.m. Deed of release; order for discharge
v.t. To liberate, release, free; to discharge, acquit; to rescue, extricate, deliver (from, -se)

ety: for A. خلعة, v.n. fr. خلع 'to put off,' &c.
def: s.m.f. A dress; a robe of honour with which princes or those in authority confer dignity on subjects; a present, a gift; — approval, commendation
more: n. m. خلع he took off from himself and bestowed upon him a garment. 1. A dress of honor bestowed by the ruling or superior authority to an inferior as a mark of distinction. Usually the khilat consists of pieces of cloth not made up, and sometimes it consists of arms, jewels, or other valuables, without any articles of attire, although in most cases a turban and shawl from part of the gift. A complete khilat may include arms, or a horse, or an elephant. A khilat is sometimes given to a person invested with a new office, or as a token of confirmation in that he holds. It is likewise presented or sent as a present by men of rank to visitors of distinction. 2. Approval of a pupil's hand-writing. Āj hamāre chār harfoṅ ko khilat milā. khilat-bahā , n. A cess, or abwāb , levied by the Mahommedan Government to defray the cost of honorary dresses presented at court. khilat pahnānā, yā denā , v. …
s. m. and f. A dress, robe of honour, with which princes confer dignity on subjects. خلعت پـﮩرانا يا دينا ḵẖilʼaﻌt -pahrānā or -denā, To invest with a robe of honour.
subs: s.m. A cess levied by the Mohammadan Government to defray the cost of robes of honour presented at court
ḵẖilʻat denā (-ko), To invest with a robe of honour.

ety: fr. خلو 'to be or become empty,' &c.
def: adj. Empty, vacant, void, desert; hollow, having nothing in it, blank, not filled up, not full; unoccupied, unemployed, free; exempt (from, -se); devoid, destitute (of); — without effect, ineffective, inoperative; — pure, unmingled; mere, only, sole, single, unaccompanied; — adv. Alone, by oneself, singly; — idly, unemployed; — s.m. (?), The month between the festivals of ʻīd and bakar ʻīd (so called on account of no festival occurring during that period)
more: adj. خلو being empty. 1. Empty; void; hollow; blank; wanting. 1. Peṭ bhī khālī, god bhī khālī . Prov. Saying. Womb and lap both empty are. 2. Khālī hāth muṅh tak nahiṅ jātā . Prov. Empty hands dont go to the mouth. 2. Not filled up; vacant. 1. Bharā kahār, khālī kumhār tez jātā hai . Prov. The laden porter and the empty potter move fast. 2. Ohdah khālī hai . The appointment is vacant. 3. Khālī jauṅḍe gireṅ pakherū, Khetī bīch bajā tālī, Jauṅḍā mat chhoṛ, purakh, khālī . Rus. Wom. The watch away, the field to birds giv'n o'er a prey, Your hands clap in the field, your perch leave not you may. 4. Maidān khālī hai . The coast is clear. 5. Khālī az lutf nahīṅ . It is not void of interest. 3. Not full; exhausted. 4. Unoccupied; having nothing to do; at leisure. 1. Tumheṅ gairoṅ se kab fursat, ham apne gam se kab khālī, Chalo bas ho chukā milnā! na tum khālī, na ham khālī . Suitors, sweet one,…

adv. 1. Alone; singly; itself. 2. Idly; unemployed. Nīṅd uchaṭ gaī, karvaṭ jal gaī, kāṭī nahīṅ kaṭtī hai rāt, Khāli khūlī kyā baiṭheṅ, ṭuk ji bahlāveṅ mil-kar sāt. Chapṭī-nāmā . Nazīr. Sleepless, I turn and turn, my sides so hot, the night wont speed, Come, let's employ the vacant hours and mutual pleasure breed. khālī khānah , A blank column. khālī denā , v. a. To parry. khālī kā mahīnā . See khālī kā chāṅd , under chāṅd. khālī k. , v. a. To empty; vacate; evacuate; exhaust; discharge. khālī-hāth , adj. 1. Empty-handed; empty; unarmed. 2. Destitute; penniless. khālī h. , v. n. To be emptied or exhausted; to fail.

(from خلو) adj. 1. Empty, vacant, void, desert, exempt or free (from). 2. Pure, mere, only, unmixed. 3. Unoccupied, unemployed, without effect. خالي کرنا ḵẖālī karnā, To empty, evacuate. خالي هاتهـ ḵẖālī hāth, adj. 1. Empty-handed; penniless. 2. Unarmed.

v.t. To empty (of, -se), to clcar or rid (of); to discharge; to vacate, to evacuate; to exhaust
(-se), To be emptied (of), to be exhausted; to be without, be devoid (of); to be free (from), &c.

more: n. m. خلف succeeding. The successor to sovereign power, applied especially to the successors of Mahomed, who united the characters of head of the State and of the Mah. religion; a caliph. In India the term is applied to a Mah. tailor, barber, and sometimes to a cook, and also to a monitor in a school, or the teacher's son.

def: adj. & s.m. Curved, twisted, bent, crooked, bowed; curled; coiled; — a bend, curve, crook; a curl, knot, ringlet; a coil, fold, ply; crookedness, curvature; bending, flexure; — the part of a noose which encircles the neck; a noose; — the upper arm
more: n. m. P. خميدن 1. A bend; flexure; curvature; curve. 2. A coil; fold; curl; ringlet. 3. A noose. kham ṭhok'nā ; Bhoj. tāṛī ṭhokal , v. a. To strike the arms preparatory to wrestling, etc. (as wrestlers do); a challenge. kham-dār, khamīdah , adj. Curved; twisted; bent; curled. kham o cham , Poet. n. m. Graceful motion or movement.
n. m. S. कुम्भ 1. A large jar; an alembic; a hogshead. 2. Each deal of four cards to three players. khum chaṛhānā , Ped. for bhaṭṭī chaṛhānā , v. a. To boil clothes preparatory to washing them.

1. adj. Twisted, crooked, coiled, curved, bowed. 2. s. m. A coil, fold, ply, curl, ringlet, bend, curve, crook, bending, crookedness. 3. That part of a noose which encircles the neck. خم اندر خم ḵẖam andar ḵẖam or خم در خم ḵẖam dar ḵẖam, Twist within twist, curly, curling locks. خم ٿهونکنا بجانا يا مارنا ḵẖam-ṭhonknā, -bajānā, or -mārnā, v. a. To strike the hand against the arms previous to wrestling, &c., to challenge (as wrestlers do). خم چم ḵẖam-cham, or خم و چم ḵẖam-o-cham, 1. s. m. Coquetry, blandishment of a mistress. 2. adj. Elegant, graceful. خم چوگان ḵẖam-i-chaugān, s. m. The bat or instrument with which the game of چوگان chaugān is played. خم دار ḵẖam-dār, adj. Twisted, curled, bent, crooked, curved.
subs: adj. Twist within twist; curly; — s.m. Curling locks; — ḵẖam bajānā, ḵẖam ṭhoṅknā (apne), To strike the hand against the arms (preparatory to wrestling), to challenge (as wrestlers do)
s.m. Graceful motion; — coquetry, blandishment (of a mistress); — adj. Elegant, graceful
s.m. The curved stick with which hockey-on-horseback is played
adj. Curved, twisted, bent, crooked, &c. ( = ḵẖam)

def: s.m. corr. of خم 'upper arm,' q.v. (for phrases).
See خم ٿهونکنا under خم kham.
s.m. (dialec.) Post, pillar, &c.; — mast (of a ship).
s.m. contrac. of khambh, q.v.
more: n. m. S. स्तम्भ; ; Pr. khambho . A pillar ( tham ).
s. m. A pillar: see کهنبهہ khambh. ¶ khum, s. m. (dakh) See کنبهہ kumbh.
adj. and s. (dakh.) See خم ḵẖam.

def: s.m. A writing-reed, a pen
more: s. m. A pen, a writing-reed. خامه دان ḵẖāma-dān, s. m. A pen-case.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَامَه (xāma, “reed; pen”).

def: s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.
more: (for کهنباچ) s. f. Name of a rāginī.

def: s.m. A species of cowhage, Mucuna nivea (the tender fleshy pods of which, when stripped of their exterior skin, make an excellent vegetable for the table, scarcely inferior, to the garden-bean of Europe).
more: n. m. Mucunanivca , Wat.; a hot weather bean.

def: s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.
v. a. (dakh.) To bow, to bend ( Harris ): see خمانا ḵẖam-ānā.
v.t. To bend, twist, crook, bow; to coil; to curl.
more: v. a. To twist, to curl, to coil, to crook.

def: s. (dakh.) Barn.

ety: Prk. खम्म = S. घर्म; s = sā, 'like,' q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Close, sultry, hot; — the hot or sultry season.
v.t. (Dakh.) = khapasnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. खंभो and खंभओ = S. स्तम्भः and स्तम्भ+कः
def: s.f. A pillar, a column; a stake, post, pole
subs: m. plur. The two perpendicular beams at the mouth of a well, supporting a third transverse beam to which the bucket-pulley is fixed.

ety: Prk. खंभो and खंभओ = S. स्तम्भः and स्तम्भ+कः
def: s.f. A pillar, a column; a stake, post, pole
more: s. m. A post, a pillar.
subs: m. plur. The two perpendicular beams at the mouth of a well, supporting a third transverse beam to which the bucket-pulley is fixed.

def: s.m. See کهمبهہ khambh, and کهمبها khambhā.

ety: Prk. खंभो and खंभओ = S. स्तम्भः and स्तम्भ+कः
def: s.f. A pillar, a column; a stake, post, pole
subs: m. plur. The two perpendicular beams at the mouth of a well, supporting a third transverse beam to which the bucket-pulley is fixed.

def: s.m. See کهمبهہ khambh, and کهمبها khambhā.

ety: Prk. खंभो and खंभओ = S. स्तम्भः and स्तम्भ+कः
def: s.f. A pillar, a column; a stake, post, pole
subs: m. plur. The two perpendicular beams at the mouth of a well, supporting a third transverse beam to which the bucket-pulley is fixed.

def: s.m. See کهمبهہ khambh, and کهمبها khambhā.
s. (dakh.) Lash ( Harris ).
s.f. Rawness, unripeness, immaturity; inexperience; imperfection, defect, fault; weakness, infirmity (of a title, &c.); — loss.
more: n. f. 1. Rawness; unripeness; immaturity. Chāṅoloṅ meṅ zarā khāmī rah gaī . 2. Defect; fault. Abhī tumhāre khat meṅ khāmī hai . Your hand is not formed yet. 3. Infirmity (in a title).
s. f. 1. Rawness, unripeness, immaturity; inexperience. 2. Loss.

def: s.f. Crookedness; bending, bend, curvature, flexure.

ety: perf. part. of ḵẖamīdan; see ḵẖam
def: part. adj. Curved, bent, crooked, awry ( = ḵẖam)
more: part. Crooked, bent, curved. خميده قد ḵẖamīda-ḳadd, adj. Bent in stature.
subs: adj. Bent in form or stature.

def: s.f. Crookedness; curvature.
adj. To be bent, fit to be bent.
s.m. Sleepiness, drowsiness, heaviness; lassitude, fatigue, weariness.
s.m. Leaven, ferment; kneaded and leavened dough; — composition, constitution; earth, clay
more: n. m. 1. Leaven (Sant. ranu ); yeast. 2. Earth; clay. Iskā khamīr yahīṅ kā thā . Of this earth he was made. (Said of the place where one dies). 3. Nature; constitution. khamīr uṭhānā , v. a. To ferment.
(from خمر) s. m. Leaven, whatever is used to ferment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خمیر (xamīr), from Arabic خَمِير (ḵamīr).

def: s.m. Leaven, &c. ( = ḵẖamīr); — conserve (of roses or violets); a thick syrup; — a kind of tobacco.
more: n. m. 1. A thick syrup. Khamīrā banafshā . 2. A kind of tobacco.
(from خمر) s. m. Leaven, whatever is used to ferment.

def: adj. Leavened; — a sort of leavened bread.
more: adj. Leavened. khamīr'ī , n. f. A sort of leavened bread.

def: adj. Much given to drink; crop-sick.
v. n. To seal up (خامنا).
v. n. (dakh.) To bend, to bow ( Harris ).
v. n. (dakh.) To bow, to bend, to decline ( Harris ): see خمنا ḵẖam-nā above.
v.t. To close (the mouth of a vessel) with earth, or flour, &c.; to close, to seal, fasten (a letter, &c.)
more: v. a. 1. To shut the mouth of a vessel with earth, flour, etc. Iskā muṅh khām do . Shut his mouth. 2. To gum; close; seal. Chiṭṭhī khām-ke ḍāk meṅ lagā do .

def: adj. Silent; — s.m. A disease in horses.
more: adj. Silent. khāmosh ; H. chup , intj. Silence!
1. adj. Silent. 2. s. m. A disease in horses.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَاموش (xâmôš).

def: adj. Silent; dumb; — intj. Be silent! silence!
more: (contract. of خاموش) adj. Silent, dumb; silence! (Pers. plur. خموشان).

def: s.m. pl. (of ḵẖamosh), The silent ones, the dead.
s.f. Silence, taciturnity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خاموشی (xāmōšī). By surface analysis, خاموش (xāmoś) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. Silence.

ety: inf. n. of خمر 'to veil or cover'
def: s.m. Covering, concealing; clouding; — fermenting, leavening; wine, spirituous or fermented liquor.
more: (from خمر Heb. To be hot, to boil, to ferment) s. m. 1. Wine, spirituous liquors. 2. Covering, hiding. 3. Fermenting, leavening.

def: adj. fem. of ḵẖamsa, 'five,' q.v. (used only in Arabic phrases).

ety: for A. خمسة ḵẖamsat; rt. خمس 'to take the fifth,' &c.
def: adj. Five; — s.m. An aggregate of five poetical pieces; a stanza of five lines.
more: adj. خمس five. A stanza of five lines.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic خَمْسَة (ḵamsa).

ety: khamas, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Closeness, sultriness, heat ( = ghamas).
s. f. Sultriness.
adj. = P خامشي ḵẖāmushī, s.f. = ḵẖāmosh, and ḵẖāmoshī, qq.v.
s.f. = P خامش ḵẖāmush, adj. = ḵẖāmosh, and ḵẖāmoshī, qq.v.
retribution; yawn

ety: kham, q.v.+yāza, rt. Zend yāś = S. यश्
def: s.m. Stretching; yawning, gaping; — stretching by way of punishment, putting on the rack; punishment, retribution, reward, fruit
more: n. m. 1. Stretching one's arms from fatigue or drowsiness. 2. Yawning ( jamāī ). 3. Retribution; punishment. khimyāzah uṭhānā, bharnā, bhugatnā, yā kheṅchnā , v. n. To suffer the punishment of one's faults or crimes; to bear the evil effects of.

s. m. Gaping, yawning, stretching. خميازه کش ḵẖamiyāza-kash, part. Gaping, stretching, yawning. خميازه کشي ḵẖamiyāza-kash-ī, s. f. Gaping, yawning. خميازه کهينچنا ḵẖamiyāza-khainchnā, v. a. To gape or yawn as after intoxication; ( met. ) to receive the punishment of oneʼs faults or crimes.
subs: ḵẖamyāza bharnā, ḵẖamyāza bhugatnā = ḵẖamyāza khaiṅćnā (2), q.v.
1˚ To stretch; to yawn; — 2˚ To suffer the punishment (of), to reap the fruit (of; — e.g. ham-ne is-kām-kā ḵẖūb ḵẖamyāza khaiṅćā).

ety: Pehl. ahu; Zend aṅhu; S. asu; cf. also S. svāmin
def: s.m. 'Lord of lords,' a prime minister.
more: n. m. A title or patronymic of Mah. nobles, especially when of Persian or Paṭhān descent; a chief headman of several villages; (Panj.) a common adjunct to Afgāṅ or Paṭhān names. Khān and khān-sāhib are also titles applied to a native physician, songster, musician ( ḍom ), etc. khān-bahādur , A title of honor. See under bahādur .
s. m. Lord, prince, a title principally used by Paṭhāns.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خان (xān).

def: s.f. A mine; a quarry; — (met.) receptacle; nest; heap, abundance.
more: n. f. S. खनि from खन् to dig. A mine (کان).
s. f. A mine; ( met. ) A nest, a heap, abundance, receptacle.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑀸𑀡𑀺 (khāṇi), 𑀔𑀸𑀡𑀻 (khāṇī), from Sanskrit खानि (khāni, “mine”), from the root खन् (khan), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kan-. Compare Punjabi ਖਾਣ (khāṇ), Gujarati ખાણ (khāṇ), ખાણી (khāṇī), Marathi खाण (khāṇ), and Bengali খাদ (khad). Doublet of کَھانِی (khānī).

def: s.m. Division (of a house), storey, flight (of rooms).
s.m. A mine.
s.f. Clink, tinkle, jingle, &c.
s.m. The sky, the air (see kha); — the hold of a ship.
more: n. m. S. खण्ड A story (of a house) ( darja , 4).
adv. S. क्षण Pālī, khano . A moment ( chhan ). Tumhārā to khan khan kā matā hai . You change from moment to moment.

1. ( खंड ) s. m. Division of a house, story, flight of rooms: see کهنڐ. 2. ( खनि ) A mine. 3. (for क्षण ) A certain day of the fortnight, as the full, change, &c. of the moon, or a name given to certain days in the lunar fortnight. ¶ khin (for खिन्न ) adj. Distressed, suffering pain or sickness.

def: s.m. A moment, an instant (cf. ćhin or ćhan); — a certain day of the fortnight, as the full of the moon, the change of the moon, &c.

ety: Prk. खाअणअं; S. खादनीयं, rt. खाद्
def: v.t. To eat, feed on; to consume; to swallow; to drink; to inhale; to take (medicine, or the air, or a bribe, or an oath); to embezzle; (met.) to get, receive; to feel, suffer, endure; to hold, contain; to harass, plague, pester (usually with jān, e.g. jān khānā); — s.m. Eating; food, meal, dinner
more: v. a. S. खाद, Pr. khāi . 1. To eat; take in; feed on; live on. 1. Noh-bhar khāyā to khāyā, mūṅh-bhar khāyā to khāyā . Prov. You have eaten all the same, whether it be a pinch or a mouthful. (Steal a pin, steal a gridiron.) 2. Khā-ke pachhtātā hai, nahā-ke nahīṅ pachhtātā . Prov. You may eat and regret, but a bath you dont regret. 3. Parāyā khāiye gā bajā, aur apnā khāiye ṭaṭṭī lagā . To pipe and song free feast when others treat, your own behind your mat-door eat. 4. khānā pīnā gāṅṭh kā, nirī salām alek . Prov. I eat and drink at my own expense, I give 'Good morning' as a salutation mere. 5. Khāë bakrī kī tarah, sūkhe lakṛī kī tarah . Prov. He eats like a goat all day, as a stick keeps thin alway. 6. Khāë na khilāë, khālā dīdoṅ āge pāë! Prov. My aunt naught gives me to eat, may she become blind and lame! 7. Khāte kamāte raho! Benediction. Earn and live! 8. Kam khailāṅ kam muë, bahut khai…

n. m. 1. Eating; dinner ( khūrāk , 2). Khānā parāyā hai to peṭ to parāyā nahīṅ . Prov. The food is another's, but your stomach is your own. (Said to one who suffers from eating too much). 2. ( Europeans ) A feast; entertainment; supper. Āj sāhab logoṅ kā khānā hai. khānā pīnā, khānā dānā , n. m. Meat and drink; food; board. khānā denā yā k. , To feast; entertain. khāne-jog ; Sant. jom lek , Edible. khāne kī nalī yā nal . ( Anat .) The alimentary canal. khā'tā , adj. 1. Eating. 2. Expensive; costly. Khātā dhan. khātā pītā ; Sant. jom purao , adj. Well-off; flourishing ( āsūdah ). khāte pīte lāteṅ chalānā, yā mārnā , v. n. 1. To be ungrateful to God. 2. To fight with one's bread and butter. khāū , adj. 1. A great eater; a glutton. 2. One who takes bribes. Sarishtedār baṛā khāū hai . The Sarishtedār is a great eater. (He takes large bribes). khāū uṛāū , adj. Prodigal; extravagant.

1. v. a. To eat; to embezzle; ( met. ) To get, suffer, take, receive, hold, contain. 2. s. m. Food, dinner, eating. کهاجانا khā jānā, To eat up, devour. کهانا پينا khānā pīnā, Meat and drink, food, board, fare. کهائي گهر کي بهانسي گننا khāʼe ghar ke bhāṅse ginnā, or کهائي تهالي مين چهيد بهانا khāʼe thāle meṅ chhed bhānā (dakh. met. for نمک حرامي کرنا) To act with treachery or ingratitude (?).
subs: (intens. of khānā), To eat up, swallow up, devour, consume; to embezzle, to make away with; to leave out, omit; to eat away, rub or wear away, corrode, &c.
v.t. To eat up, &c. (intens. of khānā, q.v.)
s.m. Meat and drink, food, victuals, fare, board; — khāne-jog, adj. Fit to eat, eatable, edible
or khāne-hārā, or khān-hārā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Eating; corrosive; caustic, escharotic; — eater, feeder, consumer, devourer; a mouth to feed.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑀸𑀤𑀺 (khādi), from Sanskrit खादति (khādati).

def: s.m. = khaṇḍ, q.v. (and cf. khan, 'a storey'). s.m. A sugar-boiler, sugar-manufacturer
more: v. n. To wear out. khinā huā , adj. ( Bot .) Imbricate.
subs: To boil down sugar to a hard consistence, to crystallize sugar

def: wanderer, vagrant; nomadic, nomad
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خانهبهدوش.

ety: prob. rel. n. fr. ḵẖān, q.v.
def: s.m. House, dwelling, place; receptacle, socket, drawer, partition, compartment; pigeon-hole (of a desk); square (of a chess-board, &c.); column (of a tabular statement, &c.); head (in an account or statement); division, class; department
more: n. m. 1. ( In Comp . chiefly) A house ( ghar ); dwelling. 2. A receptacle; drawer; a pigeon-hole. 3. A compartment; partition. Khāne khāne . 4. A division; class. 5. A department. 6. A column (of a tabular statement); a heading; head in an account or statement. khānah-ābād , intj. May your house flourish! A benediction. khānah-badosh , adj. Nomadic; wandering from place to place. khānah-badosh , n. m. lit . bearing one's house on his shoulders. 1. A nomadic tribe. 2. A man who has no fixed residence; a vagrant; vagabond. khānah-badoshī , n. f. Vagrancy. khānah-barbādī , n. f. Destruction of a house or family, chiefly by the death of its mistress. khānah-purī , n. f. Filling up of a column or form. khānah-talāshī ; H. ṭohī , n. f. The act of searching a house. khāna-i-jam'a se baṛh jānā , To exceed the amount at credit. khānah-jaṅg , n. m. A breeder of domestic brawls; a brawler. khānah-j…

s. m. 1. House, place, dwelling, chamber, room. 2. A drawer, a partition, compartment. 3. Department. (Plur. خانجات ḵẖānajāt ). خانه آباد ḵẖāna ābād, 1. adj. Prosperous. 2. (A benediction meaning) may your house flourish. خانه آبادي ḵẖāna-ābād-ī, s. f. Prosperity. خانه بدوش ḵẖāna-ba-dosh, adj. (used substantively) A traveller, a pilgrim, a gipsey, rover, sojourner. خانه برانداز ḵẖāna-bar-andāz, A prodigal, a spendthrift. خانه بردوشي ḵẖāna-bar-dosh-ī, s. f. Travelling, roving, wandering. خانه جنگ ḵẖāna-jang, A breeder of disturbances, a duellist. خانه جنگي ḵẖāna-jang-ī, s. f. Civil war, skirmish, duel, bickering. خانه خانه ḵẖāna-ḵẖāna, adj. Cellular. خانه خراب ḵẖāna ḵẖarāb, adj. 1. Ruined. 2. Base, abject; a vain, empty fellow. خانه خرابي ḵẖāna-ḵẖarāb-ī, s. f. Ruin. خانه خدا ḵẖāna-ḵẖudā, Master of a house. خانه دار ḵẖāna-dār, s. m. Householder. خانه داري ḵẖāna-dār-ī, s. f. The state of th…
subs: 'May your house flourish!' (used by way of benediction); — adj. Flourishing, prosperous
s.m. An inclosed garden, a garden adjoining a house; a kitchen garden

adj. & s.m. 'Having his house on his back'; wandering from place to place, having no settled habitation, nomadic; — one who has no fixed residence, a rover, wanderer, a traveller; a pilgrim; a gipsy; a vagrant; — a nomadic tribe
s.f. Destruction of a house or family, breaking up of a home (chiefly by the death of the mistress)
s.f. Filling in the columns (of), entry (of, -kī)
s.f. The act of searching a house, search of a house
s.m. Flowers worked on net or muslin; the work itself
To exceed the amount at credit
s.m. A breeder of domestic quarrels; a breeder of disturbances, a brawler; a duellist
s.f. Domestic or family quarrels; intestine broils, civil war; bickering; skirmish, duel, disturbance, affray
adj. & s.m. Ruined, destroyed; base, abject; — a vain, empty fellow, a good-for-nothing fellow, a vagabond, a wretch
s.f. The state of the master of a family; housekeeping
s.m. A son-in-law who lives in the house of his wife's father (by whom he is adopted as a son)
adj. & s.m. Fond of staying at home (a term of abuse)
s.m. 'Born in the house'; child of a slave, a slave
adj. & s.m. House-burning, destructive (fire); — a house-burner, an incendiary
adj. & s.m. Sitting in the house, unemployed, idle; retired; — one out of employ; a retired officer; a pensioner.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خانَه, from Middle Persian 𐭡𐭩𐭲𐭠 (BYTA /⁠xānag⁠/).

def: adj. Relating to a house, domestic, household; private, personal; — domesticated, tame; — s.f. A kept woman; a clandestine prostitute
more: adj. خانه house. 1. Domestic; internal. Khāngī umūr . 2. Private; personal; not public or official. khāngī jhagṛā , n. m. A family quarrel or feud; internal factions. khāngī muāmelah , A domestic or private affair or transaction. khān'gī , n. f. 1. A kept woman; a clandestine prostitute. Jo khāngī hai ghar meṅ ārām kar rahī hai, Pardoṅ meṅ dostoṅ se paigām kar rahī hai, Chitvan lagāvaṭoṅ se sivā dām kar rahī hai, Chupke hī chupke apnā sab kām kar rahī hai . Nazīr. Kept-mistresses all in their houses sit at ease, From curtain screens shoot messages which lovers please, Their prices raise of those who ogle with their eyes, In secret thus each her own business safely plies. 2. A native bullock cart.
(from خانه) 1. adj. Domestick, household. 2. s. f. A prostitute.
subs: s.m. A domestic affair; a private business or matter.

def: s. f. Intestine broils.
s.m. pl. (of ḵẖāna, q.v.), Houses, dwellings, &c.
s.m. Strangulation, suffocation; the quinsy.
more: (from خنق Heb. ) s. m. The quinsy, suffocation, strangulation.

def: s.m. (f. -ikā), One who digs, a digger; a miner.
s.f. Ring, clink, jingle, chink; — a high wind.
wety: From کھنکنا (khanaknā, “to clink, jingle, clash, clatter”, verb), which is from Sanskrit खणखणायते (khaṇakhaṇāyate), from खणखणाय (khaṇakhaṇāya, “to utter or give out any peculiar sound, tick, tinkle, crack”, root), which is an onomatopoeia.

r: ḵẖānaqāh, ḵẖānaqah, ḵẖāna, gāh, gah, Sūfī
def: = P خانقه ḵẖānaqah (P. ḵẖāna+gāh or gah, arabicised), s.f. orig. 'A convent for Sūfī recluses; a convent, monastery, a religious establishment for holy men.
more: n. f. A place where religious mendicants and holy men of the Mah. religion temporarily reside; a monastery; an abbey.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic خَانَقَاه (ḵānaqāh), from Classical Persian خانگاه (xānagāh), from خانه (xāna) + ـگاه (-gāh).

r: ḵẖānaqah, ḵẖānaqāh, ḵẖāna, gāh, gah, Sūfī
def: = A خانقاه ḵẖānaqāh (P. ḵẖāna+gāh or gah, arabicised), s.f. orig. 'A convent for Sūfī recluses; a convent, monastery, a religious establishment for holy men.
v.n. & t. = khanaknā, q.v. v.n. & t. To clink, ring, jingle, clank; to chink (money).
v.n. & t. To clink, ring, jingle, clank; to chink (money).
more: v. n. To jingle, to ring.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit खणखणायते (khaṇakhaṇāyate), from खणखणाय (khaṇakhaṇāya, “to utter or give out any peculiar sound, tick, tinkle, crack”, root), which is an onomatopoeia.

def: s.f. A lady, a woman of rank, a princess; title of the wife of a ḵẖān.
more: n. f. The title of a lady of rank, or of the wife of a khān ; a lady.
wety: From Classical Persian خانم (xānum).

def: digging, excavating
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit खनन (khanana).

def: s.m. pl. (of ḵẖinzīr), Hogs; — glandular swellings, or ulcers, in the neck; scrofula.
more: s. plur. (of خنزير) Hogs; ulcers in the neck or scrofula.

ety: prob. Prk. कच्छो and कच्छओ; S. कच्छः and कच्छ+ कः
def: s.m. Mud, mire, slush, slime; muddy or marshy soil, quagmire.

ety: prob. S. कर्ष+कः; cf. khoṅć, khoṅs, khaiṅć
def: s.m. A pit, hole, cavity, hollow; a notch, incision; a rent, rip; a gap, chasm; a break, hitch; delay; a turn or bend (in a road), a warp (in a wall); a loss (in trade); discrepancy (in an account, &c.); — a basket (made of twigs, or split bamboo), a pannier; a hen-coop or cage.

ety: prob. Prk. कच्छो and कच्छओ; S. कच्छः and कच्छ+ कः
def: s.m. Mud, mire, slush, slime; muddy or marshy soil, quagmire.
more: n. m. See بچها 1. 1. A pit; gap; chasm. 2. A break; hitch; delay. 3. A turn or deflection in a road; a warp in a wall. 4. A discrepancy. 5. (Dim. khāṅchī ) A basket. 6. A hencoop ( ṭāpā , 1). khāṅchā paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be missing (a link). 2. To occur (a break, discrepancy).
s. m. 1. A tray, a basket, a pannier. 2. A cage, a hen-coop.

def: s.f. A basket, &c. ( = khaṅćiyā).

ety: dim. of khāṅćā, and = khāṅćī, q.v.
def: s.f. A small basket; a small hen-coop, &c.

s.m. Piece, fragment, part, portion; division (of a house, or a country, or of the earth); subdivision, section (of a work), chapter; apartment (of a house, &c.); side, quarter; district, province, region (much used in the comp. of Indian names of places, e.g. Bundel-khaṇḍ, Anglice, 'Bundlecund'); — coarse sugar ( = khāṅṛ); — a flaw (in a jewel); — the songs of Padmāvat; — a kind of song composed and sung by washermen
more: s. m. 1. Piece, part, portion, division of a house, apartment, division of the earth, region. 2. Sugar (coarse). 3. The songs of Padmāwat.
subs: s.f. Section of a country, a state, a province, county, district
To compose a khaṇḍ or song; to recite a khaṇḍ.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit खण्ड (khaṇḍa).

def: s.m. corr. of khaṇḍ, q.v. (used in comp., e.g. Bundel-khand). s.m. A sugar-boiler, sugar-manufacturer
more: n. m. 1. A part ( ṭukṛā 1-4). 2. A tract; region. Bharat-khanḍ . 3. The songs of Padmāvat . 4. (W.) Songs. composed and sung by washermen, etc. khand kahnā , v. n. To recite or make khands , q. v.
subs: To boil down sugar to a hard consistence, to crystallize sugar

def: s.m. A sugar-boiler, sugar-manufacturer
subs: To boil down sugar to a hard consistence, to crystallize sugar

def: n. f. S. खण्ड 1. Sugar ( būrā , 2). 2. A channel from a well to a field. khāṅḍ-sārī , n. m. A sugar-boiler or manufacturer. khāṅd-sāl , n. f. A sugar factory. khāṅḍ galānā , v. a. To crystallize sugar. kachchī khāṅḍ, kachchī chīnī , n. f. Uncrystallized sugar.

ety: fr. ḵẖandīdan; rt. Zend qar; S. sur
def: adj. & s.m. Laughing, smiling; a laugh; laughter; — a laughing-stock
more: s. m. 1. Laughter, a laugh. 2. A laughing-stock. خنده رو ḵẖanda-rū, adj. Of a smiling countenance, cheerful. خنده روي ḵẖanda-rū-ī, s. f. Cheerfulness. خنده زنان ḵẖanda-zanāṅ, Setting up a laugh, laughing.
subs: adj. Having a smiling or open brow, eheerful
adj. Of a smiling countenance, cheerful
ḵẖanda-zanān, adj. & s.m. Setting up a laugh; — one who sets up a laugh.

def: s.m. Anything broken; broken rice; — a broken board or tablet.
n. m. S. खङ्ग A straight double-edged sword; a cleaver (butcher's). khāṅḍā bajnā , v. n. To clatter (arms). khāṅḍe kī dhār par chalnā , v. n. To be strictly impartial.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Shoulder: see کاندها kāndhā.

def: n. m. 1. Broken rice. 2. A broken board or tablet. khunḍā-khoṅḍā , adj. Blunt ( bhutrā , 1). khunḍe ustre se sir mūṅḍnā , v. a. 1. To shave with a blunt razor. 2. To worry.
s.m. contrac. of khāṇḍā, q.v.

ety: prob. S. खण्ड+कः; but cf. खड्गः
def: s.m. A straight double-edged sword; a cutlass; a (butcher's) cleaver; — a piece; a flake or slice (of fish, &c.)
more: s. m. 1. ( खग्ड ) A straight double-edged sword, cutlass, cleaver (butcherʼs). 2. ( खंड ) A flake or slice (of fish). کهانڐي کي دهارپرچلنا khānḍe ke dhār par chalnā (dakh. i. q. منصفي کرنا) To arbitrate, to decide (?).
subs: 'To walk on the edge of a sword'; to move very steadily and cautiously (not swaying to right or left); to administer strictly impartial justice.

def: (P. kanda arabicized), s.f.(m.?), Ditch, fosse, moat.
more: n. f. P. کنده dug, engraved; S. खन् to dig. A ditch (Sant. gaḍlā ); a moat. Mūṅh ke āge khandaq nahīṅ . Prov. No ditch before his mouth. (He talks away without stopping).
(from Pers. کنده kanda: Kāmūs ) s. m. f. A ditch, fosse, moat.

def: s.m. (Dakkhinī) A level plain (syn. maidān).
more: s. (dakh.) Plain, field: see ميدان maidān.

def: adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar, q.v. s.m. = P خانداري ḵẖān-dārī, s.f. = ḵẖāna-dār, and ḵẖāna-dārī, q.v.s.v. ḵẖāna.
s.m. A piece (as of land, &c.); a flake, slice (of fruit, &c.).
more: s. m. A flake, a slice (especially, of fish), a division or piece (of land). ¶ khunḍlā (Sans. कोटर ) s. m. Hollow of a tree.

def: s.m. Reducing (anything) to pieces, cutting to pieces; breaking; dividing; cutting, intersecting; destroying, demolishing, annihilating; interrupting, preventing, averting, removing; thwarting; revoking; rescinding; refuting, controverting
subs: v.t. To break to pieces, cut to pieces, to divide; to destroy, demolish, &c. &c.

def: n. m. Breaking, esp. of an idol.
s.m. Family, household; race, lineage, descent, house (of a prince, &c.), dynasty
more: n. m. Lineage; descent; dynasty; house ( parvār ). khāndān-i-shāhī . Royal family; dynasty.
s. m. Family, race, house (of a prince), household. خاندان کي علامت ḵẖāndān kī aﻌlāmat, s. f. Armorial bearings.
subs: Royal family; house, dynasty; — ḵẖāndān-kī 'alāmat, s.f. Armorial bearings.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خَانْدَان (xāndān).

def: adj. Smiling, laughing (at, -se); merry.

ety: prob. khātā or khattā, q.v.+aff. nā; or fr. S. खण्ड
def: s.m. A ditch; a hole, pit; a pit from which earth has been dug to make bricks or pottery, a clay-pit ( = khadānā); — an incision, a notch (in a piece of wood).
v.t. (rustic), To cause to run off, to chase away; — to pack off, despatch, send (on an errand, &c.).
more: Rus. v. a. To send on an errand (بهيجنا 1). Jeth jiṭhānī he le le sove, Mero balam khaṅdāë dio ḍhoran pe, Bis khāūṅgī jeṭh tere bolan pe! Rus. Wom. My elder brother with his wife his time doth spend, My husband sent away the cattle just to tend, I'll poison, sister, take! your taunts sore me offend.
v. a. See کهندَنا q. v.
s. m. 1. A pit, from which earth has been dug to make bricks or pottery. 2. A notch in a log of wood.

def: adj. Relating to the family, &c.; descended from father to son, hereditary, ancestral, family; of good family, of rank.
more: adj. 1. Relating to the family. 2. Descended from father to son; ancestral; hereditary. 3. Of good birth. khāndānī aurat ; H. kulīn istrī , A woman of rank. khāndānī nām , Family name.

def: s.m. = P خانداري ḵẖān-dārī, s.f. = ḵẖāna-dār, and ḵẖāna-dārī, q.v.s.v. ḵẖāna.

ety: prob. S. खण्ड+आलः or रः; Prk. खंडालो, or खंडरो (?)
def: adj. & s.m. Destroyed, ruined, in ruins, dilapidated; — a ruin, a dilapidated building or tenement; a mass of ruins (as of a village or town); — a break, gap, hole, cavity, chasm, abyss; broken or raviny ground.
more: s. m. A ruinous or broken down building, ruins of a town or house, &c.
ex: مگر اندر قدم رکھو تو سب کھنڈر نظر آئے گا۔ در و بام میں سے اب کچھ سلامت نہیں ہے۔
magar andar qadam rakho to sab khanḍar nazar āe gā. dar o bām mẽ se ab kuch salāmat nahī̃ hai.
But if you step inside, you will see everything in ruins. Nothing inside is no longer safe or sound.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi کهنڐر (khnḍr /⁠khanḍar⁠/) / کهنڐهر (khnḍhr /⁠khanḍhar⁠/), from Sanskrit *खण्डघर (*khaṇḍaghara). Equivalent to کَھنْڈ (khanḍ, “fragment”) + ـر (-ar), from Sanskrit खण्ड (khaṇḍá).

def: n. m. pl. کهندَرات 1. A tumble-down building; a ruin. 2. ( Contemp .) A house.
formal plural of کَھنْڈَر (khanḍar, “ruin”)
s.f. = P خاندار ḵẖān-dār, s.m. = ḵẖāna-dār, and ḵẖāna-dārī, q.v.s.v. ḵẖāna.
more: s. f. Housekeeping. See خانه داري under خانه ḵẖāna.

def: s.m. corr. of khanjarīṭ, q.v. s.m. The wagtail, Motacilla alba, or Enicurus maculatus; — the spotted fork-tail.
more: s. m. Name of a small bird, a wagtail.

def: oblique plural of کَھنْڈَر (khanḍar, “ruin”)
adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar, q.v.
subs: s.m. Name of a sect of Jains.

def: n. m. A sect of Jains.
adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar, q.v.
subs: s.m. Name of a sect of Jains.

def: s. (dakh.) A falcon, hawk ( Harris ).
s.m. = khanḍ, q.v. s.m. A sugar-boiler, sugar-manufacturer
more: s. m. District, province, division, section.
subs: To boil down sugar to a hard consistence, to crystallize sugar

def: s.m. corr. of ḵẖandaq, q.v. (P. kanda arabicized), s.f.(m.?), Ditch, fosse, moat.
adj. & s.m. = khaṅḍar, q.v. s.m. = P خانداري ḵẖān-dārī, s.f. = ḵẖāna-dār, and ḵẖāna-dārī, q.v.s.v. ḵẖāna.
n. m. S. कोटर The hollow of a tree.

ety: Prk. खंड+उल्लो; S. खण्ड+उलः or ऊलः
def: adj. Disturbed; turbid.
s.f. A woman given to laughing and joking with men; — a wanton, shameless woman (a term of abuse); — a coquette.
more: W. n. f. 1. A woman given to laughing and joking with men; hence, an immodest or unchaste woman. (A term of abuse). 2. A coquette; prude.

def: part. (f. -ā), Cut, torn, broken in pieces; scattered, dispersed, destroyed; broken (as faith, or allegiance, &c.); interrupted; averted, prevented; revoked; refuted, controverted; disappointed; betrayed
subs: v.t. To break, destroy; to interrupt; to prevent; to refute, &c.

def: v. a. To prevent, to interrupt, to refute.

adj. Cut; torn; broken. Yeh pustak khanḍat hai. khanḍit k., khanḍan k. , v. a. See bhaṅg k . and toṛnā , 12—14. 1. To break. 2. To interrupt; disturb. khanḍit h., yā ho jānā , v. n. 1. To be broken. Janeū khanḍit ho gayā . 2. To be disturbed, interrupted.

s.f. A woman whose husband or lover has been guilty of infidelity; a woman who is incensed at the infidelity of her husband or lover, and makes free use of abuse, but retains no malice in her breast; a suspicious, jealous wife.
more: s. f. A woman who, provoked at the infidelities of her husband or lover, is rather abusive, speaks her mind freely, but retains no malice in her breast. See Āʼīn Akbarī, Vol. III, p. 174, 8vo.

def: s. (dakh.) Skein (of thread).
n. m. A piece; slice. Machhlī ke khanḍle .

ety: khāṅḍ˚ = Prk. खंड(इ), or खंडे(इ) = S. खण्डय(ति), rt. खण्ड्
def: v.t. To break to pieces, to pound, to crush; to excavate.
more: v. a. To excavate, to pound.

ety: khand˚ = Prk. खंड(इ) = S. खण्डय(ति), rt. खण्ड्
def: v.t. To break or cut to pieces, &c. ( = khaṇḍan karnā).

ety: khand = S. खण्ड+dim. aff. olī, fr. S. र or ल+इका, with conjunct vowel o
def: s.f. = khindṛī, q.v. s.f. A patched or ragged garment (syn. gudṛī); — a baby's quilt; — a pallet, bedding (of a beggar).
more: E. n. f. Baby quilts. Nai dhoban aur kheṅdhṛī meṅ sāban! . Prov. A laundress new, for clouts soap too!

def: Khanewal (a city in Khanewal district, Punjab, Pakistan)
wety: Named after Sangar Khan, who built a castle in the area.

def: s.m. = khāg, q.v. s.m. An egg ( = ḵẖāya).
s. (dakh.) Cough, chincough ( Harris ): see کهانسي khāṅsī.
adj. (f. -ā), Tusked; having large projecting teeth.
s.m. = khaṅgar, q.v.; — 2˚ adj. = khaṅgail, q.v. s.m. Pit, shaft (of a mine).
